components.label: Algorithm Selection Task and v5 Selector

slides.title: The corpus uses five structurally diverse real-world domains
subtitles: 1,188,265 numeric arrays were extracted to maximise structural diversity.

components.label: Real data was preferred because synthetic distributions failed to converge to ge

slides.title: Labels are empirical fastest-algorithm measurements
subtitles: Each candidate algorithm is timed on a fresh copy; the minimum observed runtime becomes the label.

components.label: RAW, REV, SHUF, QBIN50, and PSORT10 force a non-trivial decision boundary.

slides.title: Sixteen O(n) structural features characterise each array
subtitles: Every feature must be cheap to compute and linked to differential sorting behaviour.

components.label: Timing features are excluded at prediction time.
A single canonical feature extractor is shared across data generation, training,

slides.title: Prediction is made before any candidate algorithm is executed
subtitles: The selector extracts features once, runs one XGBoost prediction, and chooses one portfolio member.

components.label: Measure order, runs, repetition, distribution, robust scale
The oracle is used only for evaluation; it is not available at run time.

slides.title: Model evolution reduced the problem to its deployable form
subtitles: Regression, timing features, source-aware checks, regret-aware loss, and cascade tests define why v5 is retained.

slides.title: Evaluation measures distance from SBS to VBS
subtitles: Accuracy is reported, but selector quality is judged by regret and gap closed.

components.label: Gap closed gives the fraction of the SBS-to-VBS improvement captured by the sele

slides.title: The v5 selector closes most of the SBS-to-VBS gap
subtitles: The reported result is 76.1% top-1 accuracy, 93.1% gap closed, and 89.6% zero regret.

slides.title: Accuracy alone is insufficient for algorithm selection
subtitles: A wrong label can be harmless when the selected and optimal algorithms have nearly equal runtimes.

components.label: The result is evaluated by regret and gap closed, not accuracy alone.

slides.title: Residual error is concentrated at the introsort-heapsort boundary
subtitles: Timsort has a strong structural trace; introsort and heapsort often depend on lower-level effects.

components.label: timsort is separable when runs/order are visible.
introsort and heapsort remain close in the current feature space.

slides.title: Feature importance follows structural sorting signals
subtitles: Length, repetition, and ordering account for most of the discriminative signal.

slides.title: Leave-one-domain-out testing supports cross-domain robustness
subtitles: Each fold trains on four domains and tests on the unseen fifth domain.

slides.title: Strict checks define the boundary of the reported result
subtitles: The reported result stays with v5; later variants explain leakage risk, objective risk, and the hard boundary.

slides.title: Conclusion
subtitles: Sixteen O(n) structural features are enough to support useful per-instance sorting decisions.

components.label: The selector improves practical runtime even when top-1 accuracy is imperfect.
The strongest cases are arrays where order, runs, and repetition leave a visible
The main residual weakness is the introsort-heapsort boundary, where current val

slides.title: Contributions
subtitles: The contributions are the selector, the evaluation discipline, and the reproducible artefact.

components.label: A compact pipeline combines sixteen O(n) structural features with a gradient-boo
Source-level separation, near-tie filtering, LODO testing, and regret metrics gi
One source-of-truth feature extractor, data pipeline, and exported XGBoost JSON

slides.title: Limitations and Future Work
subtitles: The limits are portfolio scope, single hardware setting, unvalidated online adaptation, and small-array overhead.

components.label: portfolio limited to three C-level comparison sorts
absolute regret tied to one hardware/software environment
LinUCB contextual bandit designed but not validated
feature extraction becomes unprofitable below about 1,000 elements
larger portfolios: Powersort, radix/bucket guards, SIMD/GPU sorts
validated LinUCB under gradual and abrupt distribution shift
domain embeddings and stricter channel-level routing evaluation
Future work extends the portfolio, adaptation, and generalisation axes while pre
